_note: DRAFT — SYNTHETIC. Ready-to-swap depthPack for the pending vaginal-cancer synthetic patient (details TBD by Pablo). Mirrors the depthPack field structure in data/patients.json. Fill every {{PATIENT_SLOT_*}} placeholder before use. Not live; scripted design content. Makes NO survival claim and asserts NO referral mandate. Evidence ids reference data/evidence.json (2026-07-18 pack).

depthPack.placeholder: DRAFT oncology depth pack (synthetic). Prepared from the 2026-07-18 evidence pack for the pending vaginal-cancer synthetic patient; replace {{PATIENT_SLOT_*}} placeholders before use. Not live. No survival claim; the >5% figure is used only as a cachexia diagnostic criterion (NUT-04), never as a referral trigger.
depthPack.headline: Weight down {{PATIENT_SLOT_WEIGHT_LOSS_PCT}} in {{PATIENT_SLOT_INTERVAL}}: the plan still treats nutrition as an afterthought.
depthPack.gap: The plan manages {{PATIENT_SLOT_DIAGNOSIS}} and its symptoms but does not document a nutrition owner, an intake history, a weight-trajectory review, or a validated screening score. Consider whether nutrition screening or a dietitian referral belongs in the plan; the patient and clinician decide.
depthPack.question: Is there enough chart evidence to refer to a dietitian now, or should a validated screening score (MST or PG-SGA-SF) and an intake history be collected first?

research.title: ASCO guideline: Management of cancer cachexia (2020)
research.cite: Roeland et al., J Clin Oncol · DOI 10.1200/JCO.20.00611 · grade A (NUT-03)
research.note: The guideline says clinicians "may refer patients with advanced cancer and loss of appetite and/or body weight to a registered dietitian" (NUT-03). It does not mandate referral at any fixed weight-loss percentage for this synthetic patient.

supporting.title: Individualized nutrition during colorectal-cancer chemotherapy: RCT (2025, small trial)
supporting.cite: Wang et al., Nutrition and Cancer · DOI 10.1080/01635581.2025.2449719 · grade B (NUT-05)
supporting.note: Small RCT (n=88) reporting improved chemotherapy tolerance and QOL and reduced adverse effects. Adjacent colorectal population, not this tumor type; effect on survival not established.

research.honesty: No RCT is verified for exactly this population (NUT-09): the evidence here is adjacent, not exact.

depthPack.quickScripted: Underdetermined: the chart shows weight and intake deterioration that meets a nutrition-screening trigger, but it lacks a validated screening score, a dietary recall, a symptom-burden grade, and the patient's preference and access. Collect those inputs or discuss a dietitian referral now; this scripted lane does not choose the timing and makes no survival claim. The patient and clinician decide.

seats.name: Oncology
seats.rationale: The weight and intake trajectory makes nutrition risk relevant during treatment, but this chart does not establish current intake, symptom burden, or a screening score.
seats.requests: A 24-hour dietary recall, a symptom-burden grade, and a documented nutrition-screening score.

seats.name: Registered dietitian
seats.rationale: Score a validated tool now — MST (≥2) or PG-SGA-SF (≥7–8) per ASPEN 2024 (NUT-02) — and screen before and during treatment. A positive screen warrants structured nutrition assessment. Weight loss >5% is the consensus cachexia diagnostic criterion (NUT-04), not a referral mandate.
seats.requests: Weight trajectory, an oral-intake estimate, and the screening-tool score.

seats.name: Pharmacy
seats.rationale: Antiemetic and symptom-control adequacy shapes intake, but current supportive-care medications and adherence are not documented here.
seats.requests: Current antiemetic regimen, adherence, and symptom control.

seats.name: Primary care
seats.rationale: Do not assign nutrition ownership from this record alone; confirm the patient's preference and access, and keep the referral a shared decision with explicit follow-up.
